ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general wafer food product class; no specific Loacker wafer cube entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A003K covers biscuits and wafers as a category; no product-specific code for Loacker wafer cube exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for commercial wafer cube snack products.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of Loacker Wafer Cubes is approximately 25 g (roughly 5–6 individual cubes), providing about 129 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Loacker Wafer Cube — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Loacker wafer cube (any flavour), cr_id F11080032, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous wafer cookie entry (USDA SR Legacy #18167) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Loacker Wafer Cube in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 76 is estimated from the closest analogous food: plain sweet wafer biscuits / cream-filled wafer biscuits (GI ~76, high category) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) and the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (76) × available carbohydrate per serving (57.99 g × 0.25 serving = 14.5 g) / 100 = 11.0, rounded to 11.1.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (30.7 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and promotes weight gain, both of which elevate blood pressure independent of sodium content.
hypertension.guidance: Despite a moderate sodium level of 166 mg/100 g, the very high sugar content (30.7 g/100 g) with zero fibre triggers insulin-mediated sodium retention per AHA 2018 guidelines; individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this product.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 30.7 g/100 g exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling; high GI (~76) and zero dietary fibre cause rapid postprandial blood glucose spikes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 30.7 g sugar/100 g, a high estimated GI of 76, and zero dietary fibre, Loacker Wafer Cubes are unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be avoided or limited to a single cube on rare occasions, paired with protein or fat to partially blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~13.8 g/100 g (likely from palm-based vegetable fat) combined with 30.7 g sugar/100 g and zero fibre raises LDL cholesterol and promotes atherogenic dyslipidaemia.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The estimated saturated fat content of ~13.8 g/100 g (approximately 50% of total fat) and high refined sugar load make this product unsuitable for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease; consumption should be minimised in line with AHA and ESC dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~13.8 g/100 g from palm-based fat and 30.7 g refined sugar/100 g with zero fibre promote pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6).
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product due to its high saturated fat and refined sugar content, both of which amplify systemic inflammation and may worsen joint symptoms during flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Loacker Wafer Cube (Any Flavour) is a bite-sized, ultra-processed commercial snack produced by the Italian confectionery brand Loacker. Each cube consists of multiple thin, crispy baked wafer layers sandwiched with a sweet cream filling, available in various flavours including vanilla, chocolate, hazelnut, and coconut. The texture is characteristically light and crunchy with a rich, sweet cream centre. Per 100 g, the product provides 514 kcal, 7.04 g protein, 27.71 g total fat (estimated ~13.8 g saturated, predominantly from palm-based vegetable fat), 57.99 g carbohydrate, 30.7 g sugar, 0 g dietary fibre, and 166 mg sodium. The product has a high estimated glycaemic index (~76) and a glycaemic load of approximately 11 per 25 g serving. Clinically, this snack is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high sugar content (30.7 g/100 g) and zero fibre, which cause rapid postprandial glucose spikes. It is also of high concern for hypertension due to insulin-mediated sodium retention from high sugar intake, and for cardiovascular disease due to high saturated fat and refined sugar. The product is widely available in Singapore supermarkets and convenience stores and is commonly consumed as a snack. It is not suitable for vegans (contains milk) and is not gluten-free. Allergens include wheat gluten, milk, and soy lecithin.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Loacker wafer cube?
Can diabetics eat Loacker wafer cubes?

Is Loacker wafer cube suitable for hypertension?
How much fat in Loacker wafer cube?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11080032, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous wafer entry and documented. GI estimated from the closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, with estimation basis clearly stated.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (13.8 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #18167 (wafer, cream-filled) as HPB Foodo does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item. This estimate is consistent with the known fatty acid profile of palm-based vegetable fat commonly used in commercial wafer products. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient, and moisture data for this commercial wafer product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. 3. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 by HPB Foodo (confirmed zero per source). 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as confirmed by HPB Foodo — this is a refined wheat flour wafer with no added fibre. 5. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on cream-filled wafer biscuits from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283); no direct GI measurement for Loacker Wafer Cube was found. 6. Halal status not confirmed from available sources; is_halal set to false pending verification. 7. This is an international commercial product (Italian origin); no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese local-language names are applicable — G6 multilingual rule does not apply.
